summary: Unconditional command execution on install and on require()
details: Package executes an arbitrary OS command both at install time (via a `postinstall` hook that loads `dist/postinstall.js`) and at library load time (via `main: dist/index.js`). Both files contain an IIFE that calls `child_process.exec` with a platform-specific command (`open -a Calculator` on macOS, `calc.exe` on Windows, `gnome-calculator || xcalc` on Linux) with no opt-in, no env-var gate, and no user interaction. The current payload is a proof-of-concept (spawning a calculator), but the structural mechanism is unrestricted shell execution on any machine that runs `npm install cache-poisoning-pwn-demo` or any package that does `require('cache-poisoning-pwn-demo')`. Import-time execution means `--ignore-scripts` does not mitigate the second path. The package author self-describes this as a cache-poisoning attack demo; the demo IS functional arbitrary code execution on installer and consumer machines.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
